title: Add conditional schema rule enforcing Evidence_Record when status=proven
anchor: definitions.CoverageItem JSON block (design.md:322-385); required list at line 324; evidence property at 381-383
change: Add an allOf/if-then to CoverageItem in the design.md schema block: `"allOf": [{"if": {"properties": {"status": {"const": "proven"}}, "required": ["status"]}, "then": {"required": ["evidence"]}}]` so a schema-valid item cannot be status=proven with no evidence object. Update the Phase-0 schema test row (design.md:623) to also assert a status=proven item with NO evidence object is rejected (today it only rejects a missing output_hash inside an existing evidence object). Add a note in Req 5.3/5.6 and Property 2 that the four-field gate is jointly schema- AND hook-enforced.

title: Resolve status/in_scope required-vs-default contradiction in CoverageItem schema
anchor: CoverageItem.required at design.md:324; status default 'unproven' at 373; in_scope default true at 378
change: A JSON-Schema `default` never populates a value for a `required` property, so 'status defaults to unproven' (Req 5.1/Property 1) and 'in_scope DEFAULT true' (Req 5.7) are not realized by the file schema even though they ARE realized by the Postgres DEFAULTs (design.md:447-448), making a hand/tool-written file that omits status schema-INVALID while the equivalent DB row is valid-and-defaulted. Pick one: (a) drop `status` and `in_scope` from CoverageItem.required and let the schema `default` annotate, OR (b) keep them required, remove the misleading `default` keywords, and reword Req 5.1/Property 1 to 'every item is WRITTEN with status=unproven by the Initializer' and Req 5.7 likewise. Note the DB-vs-file default divergence so the conservative-merge rule (tasks.md:730) stays consistent.

title: Add append-only ordering/length preservation check and property
anchor: 'Never deleted or reordered' (design.md:126, 288); Property 3 Identity-Mutation Blockade (design.md:898-902)
change: Property 3 blocks mutation of id/type/acceptance_criteria of an existing item but does NOT catch a pure reorder or a deletion that leaves surviving items' id/type/AC intact. Extend Property 3 (and the PreToolUse status/artifact guard at design.md:214-215) to diff the incoming items[] id-sequence and length against the prior committed version of feature_list.json and block on any deletion, truncation, or reorder. Add the corresponding assertion to tasks.md task 6.1 (check_artifact_guard / a new check_append_only) and its property test (tasks.md 6.4 / Property 3).

title: Extend amendment re-entry to flip the feature_list.json item, not only Postgres coverage_items
anchor: 'On amendment: insert a version row AND reset coverage_items.status to unproven' (design.md:1152); REQ-COV-007 / Property 25
change: REQ-COV-007/Property 25/CHECK-10 reset only Postgres coverage_items.status; on a Postgres-down amendment (the file is the Phase-0 source of truth and DB-down fallback, design.md:815) the feature_list.json item stays proven while the model is logically unproven. Extend Req 22 / the amendment design step / task 48 to ALSO flip the corresponding feature_list.json item to unproven and invalidate its plan-approval binding (re-block via feature_list_sha) on amendment, and add a Postgres-down amendment test under task 39.15.

title: Enforce ears_pattern (and conditionally provenance) at the file level or relocate the guarantee to spec_validator
anchor: CoverageItem.required at design.md:324; ears_pattern at 345-349; provenance at 365-369
change: `ears_pattern` and `provenance` are schema-optional, yet Req 1.4/Property 16 require exactly one EARS pattern per item and Req 2.3 requires provenance on every inferred requirement, so both guarantees are unenforced at the file level. Either add `ears_pattern` to CoverageItem.required and add an allOf making `provenance` required when provenance=='inferred' is in play (or unconditionally), OR explicitly state in Req 1.4 and Req 2.3 that these are enforced by spec_validator.py at authoring time (not by the file schema) and add the corresponding spec_validator check + task. Pick one and make schema, requirements, and Property 16 agree.

title: Add an NFR subtype field so the Verifier can route performance vs accessibility deterministically
anchor: CoverageItem schema (design.md:331-385), type enum at 331-335; Req 25.1/25.2 (requirements.md:447-448)
change: Req 25.1/25.2 route an NFR item to k6/Lighthouse vs axe-core by subtype `performance`/`accessibility`, but CoverageItem has only `type ∈ {functional,NFR,WIRING}` and no machine-readable subtype, so perf_a11y_verifier cannot select its evidence tool from the coverage model. Add an optional `nfr_subtype` property (enum e.g. {performance, accessibility, reliability, security}) to CoverageItem, required-when type=='NFR' via an allOf, mirror it in the Postgres requirements table, and reference it from Req 25.1/25.2 and the verifier component (design.md:146).

title: Tie WIRING-type items to an integration-test Evidence_Record at the schema/Property level
anchor: type description 'WIRING items require integration-test evidence' (design.md:334); EvidenceRecord schema (design.md:387-411); Req 8.3 (requirements.md:174)
change: Req 8.3 demands an integration test exercising the real execution path as the Evidence_Record for a proven WIRING item, but EvidenceRecord is a uniform 4-field shape with no marker distinguishing an integration test from a unit test, and no schema rule/Property ties WIRING→integration evidence. Add an `evidence_kind`/`test_kind` enum field (e.g. {unit, integration, behavioral, perf, a11y}) to EvidenceRecord, add an allOf requiring evidence_kind=='integration' when the item type=='WIRING' transitions to proven, and add a Property (or extend Property 2) asserting it. Wire it to task 47.

title: Block COMPLETE/merge on an empty (zero-item) feature_list.json
anchor: items array (no minItems) design.md:315-319; evaluate_stop completion gate design.md:728-736; OPA zero-evidence policy
change: tools/feature_list_init.py writes `items: []` (tasks.md:239) and the items array has no minItems, so an all-empty coverage model trivially satisfies the Stop gate (not_proven is empty → allow → COMPLETE) and the OPA zero-evidence policy, reading as COMPLETE before discovery has run. Add a precondition: gates (Stop completion path and OPA merge policy) must additionally require that discovery has run / items is non-empty (or that no domain-baseline item is UNMAPPED) before a COMPLETE verdict; document that `items: []` is a valid INIT state but not a valid COMPLETE state, and add a test asserting an empty file blocks COMPLETE.

title: Reconcile feature_list_sha raw-byte binding with the expected in-place status-flip mutation
anchor: plan-approval gate compute_sha256(read_bytes()) design.md:791-792; 'status flip is the expected mutation' design.md:288
change: The plan-approval gate binds approval to sha256 over the literal file BYTES (design.md:791-792), but the design EXPECTS in-place status flips unproven→proven (design.md:288) which change those bytes — so after the first legitimate status flip the SHA no longer matches and every subsequent implementation Write is re-blocked, forcing a fresh human approval; whitespace/key-reorder changes also re-block. Specify a canonical comparison: compute feature_list_sha over a canonicalized projection of the item set (e.g. sorted (id,type,priority,dependencies,acceptance_criteria,in_scope) excluding the mutable status/evidence fields), so status flips do NOT invalidate approval while id/type/AC/scope changes do. Update Property 6 and design.md:789-801 accordingly.

title: Define MAX_TURNS_PER_SLICE constant and reconcile run_state.budget_exceeded / token_budget_usd against the DDL
anchor: evaluate_stop pseudocode lines 699-707: `if run_state.iteration_count >= MAX_TURNS_PER_SLICE:` and `if run_state.budget_exceeded:           # token_cost_usd >= token_budget_usd`
change: MAX_TURNS_PER_SLICE is used at design.md:699/701 but never defined as a module constant (only N_PROGRESS_WINDOW=3 and SPEC_COMPLETION_HARD_CAP=7 are). Add a module-constant block near line 654 (e.g. `MAX_TURNS_PER_SLICE = 25  # DEFAULT, bound to the task-44 execution-bounds config module`). Also `run_state.budget_exceeded` and `token_budget_usd` (line 704) have NO backing column: the run_state DDL (481-496) defines only `token_cost_usd NUMERIC(10,4)`. Either add a `token_budget_usd` column to the run_state DDL, or replace `run_state.budget_exceeded` with a computed predicate `run_state.token_cost_usd >= TOKEN_BUDGET` where TOKEN_BUDGET is a config constant bound to the Requirement-20 registry (req:357). Bind MAX_TURNS_PER_SLICE and the budget threshold to task 44 (Execution-bounds config module).

title: Wire with_reentrancy_guard into a stop_hook main() entrypoint
anchor: `def with_reentrancy_guard(fn):` block at design.md:741-753 (immediately after the evaluate_stop pseudocode)
change: with_reentrancy_guard(fn) is defined but no code composes it with evaluate_stop, so the guard is dead. Add a `main()` entrypoint showing `result = with_reentrancy_guard(lambda: evaluate_stop(event)); save_run_state(run_state); sys.exit(0 if result.decision=='allow' else 2)`. This wires Req 10.4 (REQ-GATE-004) into the component. Mirror in tasks.md:100/126 (task 5.1 names with_reentrancy_guard and task 5.5 names the reentrancy unit test, but neither shows the composition).

title: Amend Property 5 and design rows 209/624 to include the HANDOFF-allow carve-out
anchor: Property 5 (design.md:914-916 'Termination is permitted only when zero items are `unproven`'); Hook-Wiring row design.md:209 'Termination while any in-scope item is `unproven` or `violation_count > 0`'; Phase-0 test row design.md:624 'With one unproven item → blocks'
change: Property 5, the Stop hook-wiring 'What it blocks' cell (209), and the Phase-0 test-to-pass row (624) all state the pure 'unproven blocks' rule WITHOUT the REQ-LOOP-005 HANDOFF override. At HANDOFF (cap/budget/no-progress) in-scope items are EXPECTED to remain unproven yet the hook MUST allow (exit 0); a naive implementer following Property 5/row-624 alone reproduces the infinite-block defect. Amend all three to read: 'blocks termination while any in-scope item is unproven AND no HANDOFF trigger (cap/budget/no-progress) is active.' tasks.md:948 already extends this caveat to Properties 8/9 — extend the same caveat to Property 5 and update the property-test spec at tasks.md:104-108 (5.2) which currently asserts unconditional exit-2 on any unproven mix.

title: Mark the Stop-hook audit_log.append call as a Phase-2 addition (guarded/no-op until task 52.3)
anchor: Hook-Wiring table Stop row, design.md:209 'calls `audit_log.append(...)` on the allow/block decision'
change: design.md:209 and Req 27.4 present `audit_log.append(...)` as always-on in the Phase-0 Stop hook, but `audit_log.py` (the producer) is Phase 2 and its wiring into stop_hook.py is task 52.3 (Phase 2). A Phase-0 hook cannot call a Phase-2 producer. Annotate the Stop row (and the SubagentStop/PreToolUse audit rows at 217/218/210) to say the audit_log.append call is a Phase-2 addition (no-op/absent until task 52.3 wires it), matching the staging already implied by tasks.md:970. Keeps the Phase-0 spine self-consistent.

title: Add a run_state.phase discriminator and specify per-phase cap/no-progress routing in evaluate_stop
anchor: run_state DDL design.md:481-496 (no `phase` column); evaluate_stop pseudocode design.md:685-737 (single undifferentiated cap/no-progress path)
change: stop_hook serves BOTH the spec-completion loop (Req 4: violation_count, cap=7 passes, pass-level strict-decrease no-progress) AND the implementation loop (Req 10/14: slice completion, cap=25 turns/slice, slice-level N=3 no-progress, design.md:840). The design never states how one Stop hook discriminates the two phases, yet they have different caps and different no-progress semantics. Add a `phase TEXT CHECK (phase IN ('spec','implementation'))` column to the run_state DDL and branch evaluate_stop: in spec phase apply SPEC_COMPLETION_HARD_CAP=7 + pass-level no-progress; in implementation phase apply MAX_TURNS_PER_SLICE=25 + slice-level check_no_progress. Cite design.md:832-842.

title: State that an in-scope item in `failed` status also blocks the Stop completion gate
anchor: evaluate_stop completion gate design.md:731 `not_proven = [i for i in in_scope_items if i.status != "proven"]` and Property 5 (design.md:914-916)
change: With Req 5.8 / Property 3 making `failed` a first-class status, the completion gate `i.status != "proven"` means a `failed` in-scope item ALSO blocks termination (it is not-proven), exactly like `unproven`. Property 5 and the Stop hook-wiring row (design.md:209) describe the gate only in terms of `unproven`, never stating that `failed` likewise blocks. Update Property 5 / the design narrative to say 'any in-scope item whose status is not exactly `proven` (i.e. `unproven` OR `failed`) blocks termination', so the implementer does not special-case only `unproven`. Cite requirements.md:129; design.md:731,900.

title: Add a live PreToolUse secret-block gate (REQ-SEC-005 / 17.5) to the hook table and task 6
anchor: Hook Wiring Table, after the `PreToolUse` — status guard row (design.md:215); and tasks.md task 6.1 check list (tasks.md:131-136)
change: Add a 7th PreToolUse sub-gate row to the hook-wiring table: `PreToolUse` — secret-block guard | Trigger: any tool call carrying a prompt/span-attribute/URL/Bash-arg | scans args+prompt against secret regexes (API keys, tokens, passwords, connection strings) | blocks (exit 2) any call that would carry a secret | REQ-SEC-005 (17.5), Property 20. Then add `check_secret_block(event)` to task 6.1's chained checks and cite 17.5 in task 6's `_Requirements:` line. This is the live prevention gate 17.5 mandates ('NOT only via an offline test'); Property 20 (offline test, task 39.11) and task 45 (CI gitleaks, 17.2) do not satisfy it.

title: Fix Property 26 dangling cross-reference 'Validates: Requirements 27'
anchor: Property 26, 'Validates: Requirements 27 (REQ-STATE-005)' (design.md:1090)
change: Change 'Validates: Requirements 27 (REQ-STATE-005)' to 'Validates: Requirements 23 (REQ-STATE-005)'. Requirement 27 is the Tamper-Evident Gate-Decision Audit Log; REQ-STATE-005 is Requirement 23 (Resumed-State Integrity), which is the requirement Property 26 / the PreToolUse integrity guard actually validates. The parenthetical REQ-ID is correct; the bare requirement number is wrong.

title: Pin the artifact guard's protected-path globs and destructive-Bash regex set canonically
anchor: Hook-wiring artifact-guard row (design.md:214) and task 6.3 (tasks.md:134)
change: Define an explicit canonical PROTECTED_PATHS glob set (e.g. feature_list.json, schema/feature_list.schema.json, tests/**, .github/workflows/**, .github/policies/**) and a DESTRUCTIVE_BASH regex set covering bypass-equivalent forms (rm -rf, DROP TABLE, git reset --hard, git push --force/-f, truncate, find ... -delete, > redirection over protected paths) in design.md, and reference them from task 6.3. Also reconcile the membership disagreement: the design hook-table row (design.md:214) lists only `rm -rf` and `DROP TABLE` while task 6.3 (tasks.md:134) adds `git reset --hard` — make the two sources agree. Add a property test enumerating bypass-equivalent destructive commands.

title: Fix systematic Property→Requirement off-numbering (Property 26 mislabeled Req 27)
anchor: design.md:1090 '**Validates: Requirements 27 (REQ-STATE-005). Machine-checked: CHECK-11...**' (and the same family: L1080 Property 25, L1100 Property 27, L1110 Property 28)
change: The canonical addendum mapping (requirements.md:507-512) makes REQ-STATE-005 = Requirement 23, but Property 26 cites 'Requirements 27' (Req 27 is the Tamper-Evident Audit Log). This mislabels THIS component's own correctness Property. Change 'Requirements 27' → 'Requirement 23' on L1090. The off-by-N is systematic across the block: Property 25 'Requirements 26' → Requirement 22 (REQ-COV-007); Property 27 'Requirements 25' → Requirement 24 (REQ-SPEC-016); Property 28 'Requirements 28' → Requirement 27 (REQ-AUDIT). Fix all four to match the requirements.md:505-512 cross-reference table.

title: Annotate SessionStart hook-wiring row + inventory that integrity compute is a Phase-2 augmentation
anchor: Hook Wiring Table SessionStart row (design.md:219) and Component Inventory rows for session_start_hook.py (design.md:129, Phase 0) and state_integrity.py (design.md:143, Phase 2)
change: The SessionStart hook-wiring row presents the hash-compute as part of SessionStart from day one, but state_integrity.py is Phase 2 while session_start_hook.py is Phase 0. A reader building the Phase-0 spine would believe resume_integrity_ok is written from the start. Annotate the row's 'What it does' cell: 'load (git/progress/coverage): Phase 0; integrity compute + resume_integrity_ok write: Phase 2 via state_integrity.py'. Mirror the note in the session_start_hook.py inventory row (design.md:129).

title: Specify the resumed-state hash inputs / algorithm / baseline-store location
anchor: Property 26 (design.md:1088-1090), Hook Wiring SessionStart row (design.md:219), state_integrity.py inventory + 'New / changed components' note (design.md:143, design.md:1180); Requirement 23 (requirements.md:437-438)
change: Neither requirements.md nor design.md defines WHICH state is hashed (feature_list.json items only? + run_state fields? + git HEAD?), the hash algorithm, or where the baseline 'durable store's recorded hash' it is compared against is persisted — yet SessionStart is credited with the compute. Add a short spec near Property 26 / the state_integrity.py note: exact byte-set (canonical JSON of in_scope feature_list.json items + named run_state fields), algorithm sha256 (consistent with the evidence/audit-log sha256 convention), and the run_state column holding the baseline hash, so SessionStart's compute is implementable from the spec alone.

title: Note REQ-STATE-003 base load is example-test-verified only (no invariant)
anchor: Property 26 block (design.md:1084-1090) and the Hook Wiring SessionStart row (design.md:219); REQ-STATE-003 / criterion 11.3 (requirements.md:221)
change: The reconciliation-added integrity half has Property 26 + CHECK-11, but the component's PRIMARY purpose — REQ-STATE-003 base load (git status + progress + coverage so a session re-orients) — has NO correctness Property and NO Z3 CHECK; it is exercised only by example tests (7.2, 16.1, 17.1). Either add a property ('for any populated feature_list.json, the SessionStart summary reports the exact unproven/proven counts') or add a one-line design note that REQ-STATE-003 is intentionally example-test-verified only (no invariant), so the absence is documented rather than an oversight.

title: Scope the base evidence-schema gate to verifier results (skip-when-no-evidence)
anchor: Hook Wiring Table row: `| `SubagentStop` | Subagent result returned | Validates Evidence_Record schema (all four fields present and non-empty); calls `audit_log.append(...)` ... | REQ-VERIFY-005, REQ-COV-006, REQ-AUDIT-001 |` (design.md:217)
change: Clarify that the base evidence-schema gate applies ONLY to subagent results that CARRY an `evidence` field (verifier results): the hook locates evidence field(s) and, when none are present (initializer/research results, which produce specs/checklists not Evidence_Records), it SKIPS the four-field validation rather than failing closed. State explicitly that REQ-VERIFY-005's 'required evidence markers' for NON-verifier subagents are enforced by the omission-declaration guard (omission_declaration field), not the four-field schema. Mirror this in tasks.md task 8.1 (add a 'skip when result has no evidence field' bullet). This resolves the universal-over-'a subagent's result' wording (requirements.md:190) vs the verifier-only schema gate (design.md:217).

title: Author a per-subagent output schema so REQ-SPEC-018 (29.3) has a concrete mechanism
anchor: Component Inventory rows for `omission_guard` (design.md:148) and `schema/` (design.md:138); plus tasks.md task 54 'add omission_declaration as a required non-nullable field to each subagent output schema (initializer, research, verifier)' (tasks.md:974)
change: Add a `schema/subagent_output.schema.json` (or per-role schemas) to the Component Inventory and a task to author it, declaring `omission_declaration` as a required, non-nullable field per requirement 29.3 (requirements.md:478). Define the subagent-result ENVELOPE once in the Data Models section with documented top-level keys (`evidence[]`, `omission_declaration`, `actor_agent`, `requirement_id`) so BOTH the evidence-schema gate and the omission_guard read the same documented JSON path from the stdin payload. Reference this schema from subagent_stop_hook.py. This closes the gap that 29.3 mandates a schema field with no schema artifact to validate against, AND the field-location drift (no canonical statement of WHERE omission_declaration lives) between task 8.1 and task 54.

title: Fix omission_guard phase label (Phase 0 -> phase of task 54)
anchor: Component Inventory row `| `omission_guard` | (capability within `.claude/hooks/subagent_stop_hook.py`) | 0 | Omission-declaration gate ... |` (design.md:148); subagent_stop_hook.py inventory row labelled '0/1' (design.md:130)
change: Re-label the `omission_guard` Phase from `0` to the phase of task 54, which is scheduled in dependency-graph wave 45 (tasks.md:923, :974) — effectively post-Phase-4, NOT Phase 0. Split/annotate the subagent_stop_hook.py inventory row (design.md:130, currently '0/1') so the Phase-0 deliverable is strictly the base evidence-schema validator (task 8), while the omission_guard (task 54), the audit_log.append wiring (task 52.3, Phase 2/3), and the OTel hook.decision span (task 36.1, Phase 3) are flagged as LATER additions. The hook is genuinely a 0/2/3 multi-phase component and the single '0/1' label understates this.

title: Add a dedicated design subsection assembling the full subagent_stop_hook.py obligation set
anchor: No dedicated subsection exists for subagent_stop_hook.py (contrast the 'No-Progress Watchdog' subsection at design.md:640 which fully narrates stop_hook.py); only inventory line :130 + wiring rows :217-218
change: Add a dedicated design subsection for subagent_stop_hook.py (parallel to 'No-Progress Watchdog') specifying: the stdin parse contract; skip-when-no-evidence semantics; ordering of the omission_guard check vs the evidence-schema check; the audit_log.append call site/ordering; and exception handling (exit 2 fail-closed). Currently the hook's full obligation set is scattered across tasks 8, 52.3, and 54 with no single assembling task, and task 8.1 tags only requirement 9.5. Update tasks.md task 8.1 to also reference REQ-COV-006, REQ-SPEC-018, and REQ-AUDIT-001, and cross-link tasks 52.3 and 54 as completing the same component.

title: Enforce Property 24 (actor_agent != implementer) at the SubagentStop acceptance gate
anchor: Property 24 (design.md:1066-1070); evidence_records.actor_agent column with 'MUST be the Verifier, never the Implementer' (design.md:473); SubagentStop wiring row (design.md:217); PBT task 39.13 (tasks.md:652-656)
change: Add an actor_agent runtime check to subagent_stop_hook.py: BLOCK (exit 2) any evidence-bearing subagent result whose `actor_agent` is `implementer.md`, enforcing Property 24 ('implementer cannot self-verify') at the acceptance choke point rather than only at the DB column (design.md:473) + property test (task 39.13). Document this in the SubagentStop wiring row (design.md:217) and add it to task 8.1 / the new design subsection. The SubagentStop gate is the natural runtime enforcement point and currently nothing tasks the hook with this check.

title: PostToolUse Semgrep-only scope (CodeQL is CI-only) is nowhere stated
anchor: Hook Wiring Table, PostToolUse row (design.md:216): "Runs lint, type check, SAST (Semgrep), wiring check on changed files"
change: Add a clause to the PostToolUse hook-wiring row (and mirror in task 18.1, tasks.md:312) stating the in-loop hook runs Semgrep ONLY for SAST; CodeQL is the CI gate (task 23 / REQ-SEC-001), not run at edit time. This prevents the glossary term Semgrep_CodeQL (requirements.md:47) and REQ-VERIFY-001 (requirements.md:186) from being misread as requiring the hook to run CodeQL. Suggested: append '(Semgrep only at edit time; CodeQL runs in CI per REQ-SEC-001)'.

title: No dedicated PostToolUse design subsection: changed-file parsing, stdout feedback schema, and MultiEdit handling unspecified
anchor: After the '`plan-approved.json` Gate' subsection (ends ~design.md:801), before '## Error Handling' (design.md:805) — mirror depth of 'No-Progress Watchdog' / 'plan-approved.json Gate'
change: Add a 'PostToolUse Feedback Hook' design subsection (parallel to the Stop-hook and PreToolUse sections) defining: (a) how changed-file path(s) are parsed from the PostToolUse event payload for Write/Edit/MultiEdit — every sibling hook documents its input parsing (e.g. subagent_stop_hook task 8.1, tasks.md:178 'Parse subagent result from stdin (JSON)'; pre_tool_use reads tool input) but task 18.1 (tasks.md:309-316) uniquely says only 'on changed files' with no stdin-parsing step; (b) the JSON/text schema of the structured feedback emitted to stdout that the agent consumes next turn; (c) how a MultiEdit batch (multiple files in one event) is fanned out to the per-file lint/type/SAST/wiring runs. Also add a matching bullet to task 18.1 spelling out the stdin payload parse step.

title: No timeout specified for PostToolUse despite shelling out to four external processes
anchor: Error Handling > Hook Failure Modes table, '`post_tool_use_hook.py` crashes' row (design.md:813)
change: Add a timeout behavior for post_tool_use_hook.py. The Req 20.1 60s validator timeout (requirements.md:354) is documented only for spec_validator.py (design.md:814) and silent for this hook, yet semgrep --config auto on a large changeset can exceed 60s. Add a Hook Failure Modes row (or extend the existing post_tool_use_hook row) and a task 18.1 bullet: apply the 60s validator-timeout default per external tool (or a dedicated post-hook budget); on timeout still exit 1 with a 'post-hook timeout' finding in the structured feedback — never exit 2, never block. This is also the concrete site to connect the N-25 'never fail-open' family note (tasks.md:730): when telemetry/DB is down (task 36.1 OTel forwarding) the hook MUST still exit 1 and not crash the edit loop.

title: PostToolUse intentionally NOT an audit-log producer — state it explicitly to reconcile with OTel forwarding
anchor: Component Inventory, post_tool_use_hook.py row (design.md:131); cross-ref audit producer list at design.md:144 (audit_log.py) and req 27.4 (requirements.md:460)
change: Req 27.4 (requirements.md:460) and task 52.3 (tasks.md:970) wire audit_log.append into stop_hook.py, pre_tool_use_hook.py, and subagent_stop_hook.py only — PostToolUse is deliberately excluded — yet task 36.1 forwards PostToolUse events to OTel, so the audit log and the OTel trace appear to disagree on whether PostToolUse events are recorded. State explicitly (in the design.md:131 inventory row or the audit_log.py row at design.md:144) that PostToolUse is intentionally NOT a gate-audit-log producer because it issues no allow/block gate decision, and that its OTel forwarding is informational feedback telemetry (paired with the 'hook.feedback' span from the OBS-003 fix), not a gate-decision audit entry.

title: Req 8.1 wiring-analysis EARS story not mapped to the PostToolUse trigger role
anchor: Hook Wiring Table, PostToolUse row (design.md:216), 'Requirement enforced' column currently 'REQ-VERIFY-004, REQ-STEER-002'
change: Add REQ-EXEC-010 (Req 8.1) to the PostToolUse hook-wiring row's enforced-requirement list and note the trigger/engine split: PostToolUse invokes wiring_checker.py on changed files (task 18.1, tasks.md:313) which performs the static call-graph/dead-code analysis and emits WIRING coverage-item candidates (task 19, tasks.md:325-331). The hook is the trigger; wiring_checker is the engine. change: The hook resolves plan-approved.json (and feature_list.json) cwd-relative, but the system deliberately runs agents inside per-slice git worktrees whose cwd is NOT the repo root — so the root-level marker is missed and the gate mis-fires. Pin resolution to the repo root explicitly (e.g. `repo_root = Path(subprocess.check_output(['git','rev-parse','--show-toplevel']).decode().strip()); plan_path = repo_root / 'plan-approved.json'`) for BOTH plan-approved.json and feature_list.json, and reconcile the inventory's `/plan-approved.json` notation (design.md:139) with the resolved path used in code.

title: State whether a non-byte amendment (Postgres-only) leaves a stale plan-approved.json validly unlocking writes
anchor: `plan-approved.json` Gate (SHA over feature_list.json bytes only, design.md:791); requirement_versions reset (design.md:1152); Property 25 Amendment Monotonicity (design.md:1074-1080); Req 22 (requirements.md:433)
change: Document the interaction between Req 22 / Property 25 amendment versioning and the plan gate: when a requirement is amended and the coverage item re-enters `unproven` by mutating ONLY the Postgres requirement_versions / coverage_items tables (not feature_list.json bytes), the feature_list_sha still matches, so the existing plan-approved.json stays valid and continues to unlock implementation writes against a now-stale coverage model. Either require amendments to also rewrite feature_list.json (forcing a SHA re-block) or have the plan gate additionally check for any post-approval amendment / unproven re-entry. State the chosen rule so the stale-approval hole is closed.

title: run_state DDL has no budget threshold column the Stop hook compares against
anchor: CREATE TABLE run_state (...) block at design.md:481-496; evaluate_stop branch at design.md:704 `if run_state.budget_exceeded:  # token_cost_usd >= token_budget_usd`
change: Add a `token_budget_usd NUMERIC(10,4) NOT NULL` (operator-set DEFAULT) column to the run_state DDL, and define `budget_exceeded` as the derived predicate `token_cost_usd >= token_budget_usd` (either a generated column or documented as computed in run_state_manager). Cross-reference the 1,000,000-token-per-slice budget (requirements.md:357, REQ-LOOP-001/002) and the design.md:823 circuit breaker. Also add the budget column to task 28.5 (currently lists only status CHECK, stop_hook_active, no_progress_n). Currently the Stop hook reads `run_state.budget_exceeded` against a threshold that has no schema backing.

title: Properties 25/26/27 cite the wrong Validates requirement NUMBER (PRD codes are right)

change: Correct the requirement numbers to match the PRD codes and the cross-reference table at requirements.md:507-509: Property 25 → 'Validates: Requirements 22 (REQ-COV-007)'; Property 26 → 'Validates: Requirements 23 (REQ-STATE-005)'; Property 27 → 'Validates: Requirements 24 (REQ-SPEC-016)'. As written they point at Req 26 (Reasoning-Loop), Req 27 (Audit Log), and Req 25 (Performance/Accessibility) respectively — a self-contradiction against requirements.md and the harness CHECK-10/11/12 PRD tags.

title: gate_audit_log append-only is convention-only — no DB-level mutation prevention
anchor: gate_audit_log DDL in the Merge Reconciliation Addendum (design.md:1156-1167); requirements.md:457 (REQ-AUDIT-001 append-only)
change: Add to migration 008 / the design DDL a DB-level append-only enforcement: `REVOKE UPDATE, DELETE ON gate_audit_log FROM <app_role>` and/or a `BEFORE UPDATE OR DELETE` trigger that RAISEs. Today append-only relies solely on the hash chain (after-the-fact tamper detection) plus convention; the DB still accepts row mutation, which is weaker than REQ-AUDIT-001 intent. Add the corresponding DDL line to task 28.8.

title: evidence_complete CHECK does not enforce the sha256 output_hash format the JSON schema enforces
anchor: evidence_complete CONSTRAINT at design.md:474-477 (`output_hash <> ''`) vs the feature_list JSON schema output_hash pattern `^sha256:[a-f0-9]{64}$` at design.md:402
change: Either add a CHECK that `output_hash ~ '^sha256:[a-f0-9]{64}$'` to the evidence_complete constraint (and to task 28.4) so the DB matches the JSON-schema format, OR add an explicit note that output_hash format enforcement is delegated to the JSON-schema / evidence_collector layer. As-is, a malformed hash passes the DB CHECK (non-empty only) but fails JSON-schema validation — a DB/JSON enforcement asymmetry.

title: No index DDL anywhere; migrations component lacks an index/performance design narrative
anchor: Postgres Schema section (design.md:425-509) and inventory row `db/migrations/` (design.md:140); Property 22 OPA COUNT against evidence_records (design.md:1052)
change: Add a Migrations design subsection (apply order, owning Postgres role, required indexes) and add the index DDL to the relevant 28.x tasks. At minimum: index on `evidence_records.requirement_id` (Property 22 per-requirement COUNT), `traceability_links.requirement_id` (queried in both directions un-indexed), and `coverage_items.requirement_id`. The current schema has zero CREATE INDEX statements.

title: Postgres store cannot reconstruct acceptance_criteria / dependencies / priority — REQ-16.3 fidelity gap
anchor: requirements DDL design.md:431-440 (has priority, lacks acceptance_criteria & dependencies) and coverage_items DDL design.md:443-451 (lacks priority & dependencies) vs feature_list JSON schema required set `[id, type, priority, dependencies, acceptance_criteria, status, in_scope]` at design.md:324; REQ-16.3 at requirements.md:298
change: Resolve the source-of-truth fidelity gap one of two ways: (a) add `acceptance_criteria` and `dependencies` storage (TEXT[]/JSONB columns or a child table) to requirements, and add `priority`+`dependencies` to coverage_items, so the DB can reconstruct Property 1 (≥1 acceptance criterion) and Property 7 prior-slice ordering (priority/dependencies, design.md:336-358, 932); OR (b) explicitly scope REQ-16.3 (requirements.md:298) so feature_list.json is canonical for acceptance criteria/dependencies/priority and Postgres mirrors only the coverage-status subset. Pick one and make design + tasks + requirements consistent. This single root cause covers both the missing acceptance_criteria and the missing slice-ordering dependencies/priority.

title: Property 22 prose claims a Postgres COUNT but the built Conftest policy reads feature_list.json
anchor: Property 22 EARS prose at design.md:1052 (`SELECT COUNT(*) FROM evidence_records WHERE requirement_id = ANY(approved_req_ids)`) vs task 21.1 (tasks.md:355 'Accept feature_list.json as Conftest input; policy reads items[_].status') and Phase-0 stub task 14.1 (tasks.md:264-265)
change: Reconcile Property 22's store: either (a) rewrite the Property 22 prose so the gate is expressed over the feature_list.json embedded evidence object that Conftest actually reads (since no task wires Conftest to Postgres), keeping the evidence_records SQL as an optional/secondary check; OR (b) add a task that exports/queries evidence_records into the Conftest input. As written, design asserts a SQL COUNT against evidence_records that the realized merge gate never performs — feature_list.json is the store the gate queries.

title: Property 22 counts approved requirements without excluding out-of-scope (Req 5.7) and evidence_records has no in_scope linkage
anchor: Property 22 prose design.md:1050-1052 (counts over approved_req_ids, no in_scope filter) vs Req 5.7 'all completion and Stop gates SHALL count ONLY in_scope items' (requirements.md:128); coverage_items.in_scope at design.md:448
change: Make Property 22 honor Req 5.7: restrict the approved-requirement set (and the evidence count) to in_scope coverage items by joining to coverage_items.in_scope (or pre-filtering approved_req_ids to in_scope ones). evidence_records itself has no in_scope column, so the query must reach in_scope via coverage_items. Without this, an out-of-scope-but-approved requirement with zero evidence either wrongly fails the merge or the gate silently ignores the in_scope rule.

title: in_scope flip to false has no DB-level enforcement or audit of the human-authored transition
anchor: coverage_items.in_scope comment design.md:448 ('leaves scope only via a human-authored transition'); requirements.md:128 (human-authored only); no DB mechanism exists
change: Specify enforcement for the 'human-authored only' in_scope=false rule: either document that the hook/audit layer captures every in_scope flip into gate_audit_log (actor + justification), or add a DB trigger requiring an actor/justification on UPDATE that sets in_scope=false. Today the DB accepts any UPDATE flipping in_scope, and since gates count only in_scope items an unguarded flip silently drops a requirement from all completion gates.

title: Error Handling 'Postgres unavailable' row is thinner than the N-25 fallback contract it should mirror
anchor: Error Handling table row 'Postgres unavailable | Fall back to feature_list.json as sole source of truth for gate decisions. Log warning to OTel.' (design.md:815)
change: Expand the design.md:815 Error Handling row (or cross-reference tasks.md:730 N-25) so the design states the full fallback contract that already lives in tasks: every gate must reach the SAME allow/block decision on file-backed state (never fail-open), DB and file are kept as duplicates, the more conservative (blocking) result wins on divergence, and the gate_audit_log producer appends to a file-backed log when the DB is down and reconciles on reconnect. The requirement-level contract is captured in tasks.md:730 but the design Error Handling row reduces it to a one-liner.

title: design.md Property 25 cites wrong requirement number AND non-canonical CHECK token
anchor: **Validates: Requirements 26 (REQ-COV-007). Machine-checked: CHECK-10 (`formal_verification_merged.py`).**
change: Rewrite the Property 25 Validates line (design.md:1080) to: '**Validates: Requirement 22 (REQ-COV-007). Machine-checked: CHECK-10a/10b (`formal_verification_merged.py`).**' — REQ-COV-007 is Requirement 22 (requirements.md:409,432,507), NOT Requirement 26 (which is REQ-OBS-006 Reasoning-Loop Detection, requirements.md:413). The canonical Z3 identifier is the CHECK-10a/10b pair (formal_verification_merged.py:422,430; requirements.md:507), not the bare 'CHECK-10'.

title: Amendment status-reset (proven→unproven) is blocked by the PreToolUse status guard's permitted-edge set
anchor: | `PreToolUse` — status guard | Any write to `feature_list.json` | Validates proposed status transition; checks evidence schema completeness *(Reconciliation 2026-06-15: `unproven→failed` and `failed→unproven` are now allowed; only `*→proven` requires a complete Evidence_Record)* | Transitions into `proven` without a complete Evidence_Record; any transition not in {`unproven→proven`, `unproven→failed`, `failed→unproven`} | REQ-COV-002, REQ-COV-003, REQ-COV-006 |
change: The amendment flow resets `coverage_items.status` from `proven` to `unproven` (design.md:1078,1152; Property 25), but `proven → unproven` is NOT in the permitted-edge set {unproven→proven, unproven→failed, failed→unproven} listed at design.md:215, design.md:374, design.md:900, requirements.md:123, and requirements.md:129 — so the status guard would BLOCK the amendment reset. Add `proven → unproven` (human-authored / amendment-triggered) to the permitted-transition set in all five locations, scoped so it is allowed only on a requirement-amendment event (e.g. gated on a corresponding requirement_versions insert), and update Property 3 accordingly.

title: Amendment version monotonicity and initial-version seeding are asserted but not mechanized
change: Property 25 / design.md:1078 require a 'monotonically increasing version', but the only DDL constraint (UNIQUE (requirement_id, version), design.md:1150) permits gapped/out-of-order sequences (e.g. insert version=5 then version=3). Specify the mechanism near design.md:1150 and in task 28.7: compute `version = COALESCE(MAX(version),0)+1` inside the amendment_handler transaction (or a BEFORE INSERT trigger enforcing version = previous_max+1). Also specify seeding semantics: state whether the table holds amendments only (first amendment is version 1, its `prior_text` = the original approved text from requirements.id, hence `prior_text` is non-null after the first amendment) and what a null `prior_text` means (reserve for an optional seed/baseline row, or forbid null in practice).

title: Append-only 'never overwritten' guarantee has no DDL or named application-layer enforcement
anchor:
-- On amendment: insert a version row AND reset coverage_items.status to 'unproven'.
-- Completion gate already blocks COMPLETE while any item is 'unproven' (CHECK-10a).
change: design.md:1078 says prior version text 'shall be retained and never overwritten', but requirement_versions has no append-only protection (no REVOKE UPDATE/DELETE, no BEFORE UPDATE/DELETE trigger). Add to the migration spec EITHER an append-only guard (REVOKE UPDATE, DELETE ON requirement_versions, or a trigger that RAISEs on UPDATE/DELETE) OR an explicit note that append-only is enforced at the application layer, naming where (e.g. amendment_handler is the sole writer and never updates). Document alongside design.md:1152.

title: FK ON DELETE behavior and requirement→coverage_item reset mapping unspecified
anchor:
requirement_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES requirements(id),
    version        INTEGER NOT NULL,
change: design.md:1143's `REFERENCES requirements(id)` has no ON DELETE clause (defaults to NO ACTION). Decide and document ON DELETE RESTRICT (history must survive deletion of the requirement). Also clarify the join the amendment relies on: the row references `requirement_id`, but the status reset targets coverage_items — state the cardinality (requirement_id → coverage_item(s), 1:1 or 1:many) and how amendment_handler locates the coverage_item(s) to reset. Add an index on `requirement_id` for amendment-history lookups. Add near design.md:1143-1152.

title: Pin the write protocol that supplies DB-assigned seq/created_at to the hash producer
anchor: design.md:1173 `--   * Producer: tools/audit_log.append(event, tool, decision, reason, requirement_id, actor_agent),` (and requirements.md:461 Requirement 27.5; tasks.md:968 task 52.1)
change: Resolve the seq/created_at chicken-and-egg: entry_hash is defined to INCLUDE seq (BIGSERIAL, DB-assigned) and created_at (DEFAULT now(), DB-assigned) but append(event,tool,decision,reason,requirement_id,actor_agent) receives neither, so the hash cannot be computed before INSERT without an UPDATE (violating append-only). Pin ONE mechanism in design.md:1168-1177, requirements.md:461 (Req 27.5), and task 52.1: have append() pre-assign seq via `SELECT nextval('gate_audit_log_seq_seq')` and set created_at client-side, then INSERT the row with those exact values so the stored seq/created_at equal the hashed ones; OR drop seq+created_at from the canonical hashed tuple. State the chosen mechanism so audit_log.py and audit_verify.py compute identical hashes.

title: Pin the NULL/None canonical-JSON serialization rule for the hash
anchor: design.md:1170-1172 `--   * Canonical form = deterministic JSON of (seq, event_name, tool_name, decision, reason, requirement_id, actor_agent, created_at).` (and requirements.md:461 Req 27.5)
change: tool_name, reason, requirement_id are nullable (design.md:1159,1161,1162) but the canonical-JSON serialization of NULL is never pinned (omit key? json null? empty string?), so producer and verifier may serialize NULL differently and produce divergent entry_hash (spurious tamper failures). Pin in design.md:1168-1172 and Req 27.5 (requirements.md:461): keys ALWAYS present, NULL serialized as JSON `null` literal, `sort_keys=true`, UTF-8, no insignificant whitespace (compact separators), and require audit_log.py (task 52.1) and audit_verify.py (task 52.2) to import ONE shared canonicalizer function.

title: Decide FK from gate_audit_log.requirement_id to requirements(id), or document the exception
anchor: design.md:1162 `    requirement_id TEXT,` (inside CREATE TABLE gate_audit_log)
change: Every sibling table FKs requirement_id to requirements(id) (design.md:445,456,467,484,1143) but gate_audit_log.requirement_id is bare nullable TEXT, so a decision can reference a non-existent requirement and still persist — weakening REQ-AUDIT-001 traceability. Either add `requirement_id TEXT REFERENCES requirements(id)` to the DDL (design.md:1162) and task 28.8, OR add a one-line comment in the DDL stating audit rows intentionally allow dangling/absent requirement_id (e.g. plan-gate decisions logged before any requirement is active) so the omission is a documented choice, not drift.

title: Correct shuffled requirement numbers in design.md Property 25–28 'Validates' lines
anchor: design.md:1110 `**Validates: Requirements 28 (REQ-AUDIT-001/002). ...**` (and design.md:1080 Property 25, :1090 Property 26, :1100 Property 27)
change: The Property 25–28 'Validates' lines carry shuffled bare requirement numbers that contradict requirements.md headings and the requirements.md:507-510 matrix: design.md:1080 Property 25 says 'Requirements 26' but amendment/REQ-COV-007 = Requirement 22 (26 = Reasoning-Loop Detection); design.md:1090 Property 26 says 'Requirements 27' but resumed-state/REQ-STATE-005 = Requirement 23 (27 = the audit log); design.md:1100 Property 27 says 'Requirements 25' but checklist/REQ-SPEC-016 = Requirement 24 (25 = Perf/A11y); design.md:1110 Property 28 says 'Requirements 28' but audit/REQ-AUDIT = Requirement 27 (28 = Capability-Role Taxonomy). The parenthetical REQ-* tags are correct; only the numbers are wrong. Fix to: Property 25 -> Requirement 22, Property 26 -> Requirement 23, Property 27 -> Requirement 24.1, Property 28 -> Requirement 27 — matching requirements.md:431/435/440/455 and the matrix at requirements.md:507-510. (Property 28 line is the one directly on this component.)

title: Add verification-access INDEX(es) to gate_audit_log for chain-walk and lookups
anchor: design.md:1156-1167 (CREATE TABLE gate_audit_log) and tasks.md:701 (`Run python3 tools/audit_verify.py to recompute the hash chain over gate_audit_log`)
change: audit_verify.py and the required audit-chain-verify CI check (tasks.md:700-701) full-scan gate_audit_log ordered by seq on an unbounded append-only table (one row per gate decision, written by all three hooks on every allow/block per design.md:209-217). ORDER BY seq is index-backed by the BIGSERIAL PK, but there is no index for per-session or per-requirement_id audit queries, and no spec statement that whole-table re-hash at merge is acceptable at scale. Add to the design.md DDL block and task 28.8 a `CREATE INDEX ON gate_audit_log (requirement_id)` (and/or a session/created_at index), or state explicitly that bounded whole-chain re-hash at merge is acceptable given the retention policy.

title: Pin the resumed-state hash algorithm (inputs, serialization, hash function)
anchor: ## Merge Reconciliation Addendum (canonical merge) / New / changed components bullet: "- `state_integrity.py` (Phase 2) — recompute resumed-state hash on `SessionStart`; block proceed on mismatch (REQ-STATE-005 / CHECK-11)." (design.md:1180); and the SQL hash-chain block at design.md:1168-1176 as the sibling pattern to mirror
change: Add a 'Resumed-State Hash' spec subsection (sibling to the gate_audit_log hash-chain spec at design.md:1168-1176) pinning: (a) exact input set — recommend the deterministic JSON of the durable run_state progress fields (current_item_id, iteration_count, no_progress_n, last_commit_sha) PLUS the feature_list.json content SHA PLUS last_commit_sha; (b) canonical serialization = deterministic/sorted-key JSON; (c) state_hash = sha256(canonical_form); (d) WHERE the baseline is recorded (a new run_state.state_hash TEXT column) and WHEN it is written (each PreCompact checkpoint and each per-Slice commit). Currently 'recomputes the resumed-state hash' is asserted in design.md:143/219/1088/1180 with zero algorithm, so the SessionStart recompute and the recorded baseline cannot be guaranteed to hash identical inputs.

title: Add run_state.state_hash baseline column + a producer that records it
anchor: -- run_state: per-session execution state for resumption (design.md:481-496); specifically the column list ending '... resume_integrity_ok BOOLEAN, ... last_commit_sha TEXT,' — no state_hash / recorded-hash column exists
change: Add a `state_hash TEXT` column to the run_state DDL (design.md:481-496) to hold the recorded baseline that state_integrity.py compares its recompute against — requirements.md:437 says it compares to 'the durable store's recorded hash' but no column holds it. ALSO name the PRODUCER and moment: the PreCompact hook-wiring row (design.md:220, currently 'Checkpoints claude-progress.txt, current evidence state, and feature_list.json to git') and the per-Slice commit must additionally WRITE run_state.state_hash. Without an assigned producer the 'durable store's recorded hash' is never created by any specified writer (the comparison input does not exist).

title: Fix systematic Property→Requirement mis-numbering (Properties 25-28)
anchor: Property 'Validates' lines: design.md:1080 'Validates: Requirements 26 (REQ-COV-007)'; design.md:1090 'Validates: Requirements 27 (REQ-STATE-005)'; design.md:1100 'Validates: Requirements 25 (REQ-SPEC-016)'; design.md:1110 'Validates: Requirements 28 (REQ-AUDIT-001/002)'
change: Off-by-N requirement numbers. Per the authoritative cross-reference table (requirements.md:505-512): Property 25 validates Requirement 22 (not 26); Property 26 validates Requirement 23 (not 27); Property 27 validates Requirement 24.1 (not 25); Property 28 validates Requirement 27 (not 28). Fix all four 'Validates' lines. (Property 29→24.2 at design.md:1120 and Property 30→29 at design.md:1130 are already correct — do not touch.) This is broader than the single Property-26 collision the draft flagged.

title: Stop overstating CHECK-11 as machine-checking state_integrity.py
anchor: Component-inventory row design.md:143: "`state_integrity.py` ... (REQ-STATE-005 / CHECK-11)." and the addendum bullet design.md:1180: "recompute resumed-state hash on `SessionStart`; block proceed on mismatch (REQ-STATE-005 / CHECK-11)."
change: CHECK-11a/11b (formal_verification_merged.py:444-461) is a 2-Boolean abstraction over `resumeHashMismatch`/`runProceeds` proving only Implies(mismatch, ¬proceeds); it never references state_integrity.py, resume_integrity_ok, the hash inputs, or the SessionStart-computes/PreToolUse-enforces split. Reword design.md:143 and design.md:1180 to 'logic-validated by CHECK-11a/11b; implementation/wiring validated by Property 26 PBT (tasks.md task 39.16)', mirroring the honest Property 28 caption 'no Z3 check — runtime/CI verified' (design.md:1110).

title: Scope design.md:143 row to state_integrity.py only (non-blocking; flag-based block)
anchor: Component-inventory row design.md:143: "Recomputes the resumed-state hash on `SessionStart` and writes `run_state.resume_integrity_ok`; the PreToolUse integrity guard blocks on mismatch (REQ-STATE-005 / CHECK-11)."
change: The row conflates two components and implies state_integrity.py blocks 'on mismatch'. It does not: per design.md:212 the PreToolUse guard blocks when run_state.resume_integrity_ok == false (a flag), not on a live recompare. Reword to scope the row to state_integrity.py only ('computes the hash and writes the flag; non-blocking') and let the existing PreToolUse-integrity-guard row (design.md:212) own the block, stated as 'blocks when resume_integrity_ok == false' rather than 'on mismatch'.

title: Specify the operator-reconciliation / unblock mechanism
anchor: ## Error Handling → ### Hook Failure Modes table (design.md:805-816), alongside the existing fail-closed rows ('plan-approved.json missing or corrupt | PreToolUse blocks (fail closed)')
change: requirements.md:438 says the guard releases 'only after operator reconciliation flips it true' but no file defines HOW. Add an Error-Handling entry defining a concrete reconciliation entry point, e.g. `python3 tools/state_integrity.py --reconcile --session <id>` that re-records run_state.state_hash from the current accepted state and sets resume_integrity_ok=true, and state what reconciliation entails (re-recording the durable baseline hash). The unblock path is currently a dangling obligation.

title: Define first-run / no-baseline behavior so the guard never blocks a fresh run
anchor: run_state DDL line design.md:492: "resume_integrity_ok BOOLEAN, -- ... PreToolUse integrity guard blocks the first write when FALSE (REQ-STATE-005)" (plain BOOLEAN, no SQL DEFAULT) and PreToolUse integrity-guard row design.md:212
change: resume_integrity_ok is a plain BOOLEAN with no default; on a fresh (non-resumed) session there is no recorded baseline, so the comparison is undefined and a NULL/FALSE flag would make the PreToolUse guard block the first legitimate write. State explicitly that on a session with no prior durable state_hash, state_integrity.py sets resume_integrity_ok=TRUE (and/or the PreToolUse guard at design.md:212 treats NULL/absent-baseline as ALLOW), so only an actual recorded-vs-recomputed mismatch blocks. Mirror this with a unit/property case in task 39.16 asserting a first-run session is never blocked by the integrity guard.

title: Add a 'first write of a resumed session' detection mechanism to run_state
anchor: PreToolUse integrity-guard row design.md:212 ('First `Write`/`Edit` of a resumed session ...') and Property 26 design.md:1088 (scoped to 'the first Write/Edit of a resumed session'); run_state DDL design.md:481-496 (no is_resume / first_write_done / session-origin column)
change: The guard is scoped to the FIRST implementation write of a RESUMED session, but the PreToolUse hook is registered with no matcher (design.md:168-173, fires on every tool) and run_state has no field letting the guard recognize that moment. Add durable state — e.g. an `is_resume BOOLEAN` (set by SessionStart when a prior run_state row exists) and a `first_write_done BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE` (set true after the guard passes the first write) — and have task 49.1's guard logic key on (is_resume AND NOT first_write_done). Otherwise the guard cannot implement the 'first write of a resumed session' scope that Property 26 and Requirement 23.2 require.

title: Resolve design-internal contradiction: spine row 4 vs hook-wiring row on SessionStart duties
anchor: Phase 0 Component List spine row 4 (design.md:626): "SessionStart hook | ... | Loads git status + progress file + coverage model | ..." — no integrity computation; contradicts hook-wiring row design.md:219 and Property 26 design.md:1088 which say SessionStart computes the resumed-state hash
change: design.md is internally inconsistent about the SessionStart hook's responsibilities: spine row 4 (design.md:626) lists only 'Loads git status + progress file + coverage model' (matching task 7.1) while design.md:219 and Property 26 say it also computes the resumed-state hash and writes resume_integrity_ok. Update spine row 4 to add 'computes the resumed-state hash via state_integrity.py and writes run_state.resume_integrity_ok (non-blocking)' so the spine row agrees with design.md:219.

title: Add a 'Resumed-State Integrity' design prose subsection covering failure modes
anchor: ## Error Handling (design.md:805-816) — there is no dedicated 'Resumed-State Integrity' narrative subsection (contrast the dedicated 'No-Progress Watchdog' (design.md:640) and 'plan-approved.json Gate' (design.md:758) subsections)
change: state_integrity.py — the sole guard against corrupted resume — has no narrative subsection, only three table rows + one Property + a one-line addendum bullet; failure modes (hash recompute fails, durable baseline absent on first run, Postgres down at SessionStart) are discussed nowhere. Add a 'Resumed-State Integrity' subsection covering: hash inputs (cross-ref the hash-algo spec), where/when the baseline state_hash is stored, first-run no-baseline behavior (default resume_integrity_ok TRUE / treat NULL as allow), and SessionStart-time DB-unavailable behavior (fall back to file-backed hash per tasks.md:730).

title: Add an Error-Handling row for audit_log.append failure (fail-open-for-availability + file fallback)
anchor: ## Error Handling → ### Hook Failure Modes table (lines 809-816), last row 'plan-approved.json missing or corrupt'
change: Add a new row to the Hook Failure Modes table: '| audit_log.append fails (DB write error / hash-compute error) | Append to the file-backed fallback log (per Notes N-25 / tasks.md:730) and DO NOT block the originating gate decision — audit is a durable side-record, not a new availability gate; emit an OTel warning and mark the chain as needing reconciliation on reconnect. |'. This makes the fail-open-for-availability-vs-file-fallback policy explicit; today every other producer/hook has a failure-mode row but gate_audit_log / audit_log.py appears nowhere in the section.

title: Annotate PreToolUse sub-gate rows (or add a blanket rule) as audit_log.append producers
